lineage: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
That we receive more as the reward of our suffering than what we endure here in the suffering itself.

title: That we receive more as the reward of our suffering than what we endure here in the suffering itself.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: That we receive more as the reward of our suffering than what we endure here in the suffering itself,

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: The blessed Apostle Paul proves; who by the divine condescension, being caught up into the third heaven and into paradise, testifies that he heard unspeakable words, who boasts that he saw Jesus Christ by the faith of sight, who professes that which he both learnt and saw with the greater truth of consciousness, and says: “The sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the coming glory which shall be revealed in us.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Who, then, does not with all his powers labour to attain to such a glory that he may become the friend of God, that he may at once rejoice with Christ, that after earthly tortures and punishments he may receive divine rewards?

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: If to soldiers of this world it is glorious to return in triumph to their country when the foe is vanquished, how much more excellent and greater is the glory, when the devil is overcome, to return in triumph to paradise, and to bring back victorious trophies to that place whence Adam was ejected as a sinner, after casting down him who formerly had cast him down; to offer to God the most acceptable gift—an uncorrupted faith, and an unyielding virtue of mind, an illustrious praise of devotion; to accompany Him when He shall come to receive vengeance from His enemies, to stand at His side when He shall sit to judge, to become co-heir of Christ, to be made equal to the angels; with the patriarchs, with the apostles, with the prophets, to rejoice in the possession of the heavenly kingdom!

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Such thoughts as these, what persecution can conquer, what tortures can overcome?

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: The brave and stedfast mind, founded in religious meditations, endures; and the spirit abides unmoved against all the terrors of the devil and the threats of the world, when it is strengthened by the sure and solid faith of things to come.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: In persecutions, earth is shut up, but heaven is opened; Antichrist is threatening, but Christ is protecting; death is brought in, but immortality follows; the world is taken away from him that is slain, but paradise is set forth to him restored; the life of time is extinguished, but the life of eternity is realized.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: What a dignity it is, and what a security, to go gladly from hence, to depart gloriously in the midst of afflictions and tribulations; in a moment to close the eyes with which men and the world are looked upon, and at once to open them to look upon God and Christ!

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Of such a blessed departure how great is the swiftness!

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: You shall be suddenly taken away from earth, to be placed in the heavenly kingdoms.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: It behoves us to embrace these things in our mind and consideration, to meditate on these things day and night.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: If persecution should fall upon such a soldier of God, his virtue, prompt for battle, will not be able to be overcome.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Or if his call should come to him before, his faith shall not be without reward, seeing it was prepared for martyrdom; without loss of time, the reward is rendered by the judgment of God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: In persecution, the warfare,—in peace, the purity of conscience, is crowned.
